Trying to ride at least 50km this weekend, prepping to go for a multi day ride. This segment was chill exploratory. Nice triangle park on the top left of the route, would visit again.

Actually trying to keep somewhat of a pace in this one. Started with Holyrood climb for extra torture. Hit the longest red lights possible ♥️

Kinda overdid it and kinda forgot how early it gets dark. Amazing route for night riding if you're into spooky shit.  Some parts road quality is so bad this could count as a gravel ride.

chillest ride in a while, mostly segregated lane, felt safer than in Edi. Also, so flat.
Leg felt funky.
i cheated - pedalled standing for a few bits

with Jacob! featuring many roadies overtaking

Grippy af. But not spinny enough :/
Some parts of route did not dare send it on descent and did a walk of shame. MTBer yeeted past.
Oh and got stuck behind a ghost bus on the nice downhill home :(((

Perfect weather for all days before and after this ride. I sure know how to pick 'em. 
Very nice infra, very nice lightning, very nice rental road bike (except for the rim brakes).

It's surprisingly fine cycling completely soaked when it's warm.
weather got good so I had to go out again while bike rental time wasn't over

With Dad! Slowed him down less than usual this time!
Testing out a (relatively) newly paved cycling path, absolutely amazing surface, combined with lovely Lithuanian flatness.

column11: Without editing out bugged out GPS total distance was 143 km

column5: Because it was the only stretch of (shared) cycle path around town.

meandering around
this time was spooked by road
Chain fell off twice :D

Took out my city bike out the city limits for once.
It croaked it creaked but it did not get a puncture.
Somehow didn't die. Weather was perfect. Vibes were great, everyone on the same page, some people cheering along the way. Jacob didn't drop me despite everything ❤️
kill me
I forgor to resume the ride tracking again

Stole route from Edinburgh Gravel Society/Group/Thing. Thought to do it solo to not get dropped.
Chunky as hell gravel, was impressed with my bike just... going through it. In parts thought whoever made the route was trying to kill me :)) 
After spookiest parts got too brave and ate shit on a descent, fell into some spiky bushes. Skill Issue. Broke glasses and shoulder feels funny. Bike was okay, needed to realign handlebars and put chain back on and managed to return. 
Enjoyed climbs more than descents
hehe no glasses 🤓 🤪
One foot fine, other in pain. I might have wonky feet :(

Rare amazing weather
 One (1) cloud in the sky
thought legs don't hurt bad enough after hike
wet wet wet wet wet
perfect weather to suffer
Longer one with a break in the middle. Perfect weather. Forgot to start/resume tracking as always.
another ride another forgotten tracking resume. persistent headwind on the way back. felt legs even tho short distance... skill issue
no stop but a slowdown for the geese

Saw someone cycle on it from car window so wanted to be the one cycling on it. Accidentally ended up being a lovely and majestic ride.
Lucked out and everyone was super polite, including drivers.
On the way back realised almost all the way was a false flat (it was downhill back).
Legs were a bit tired from last few days so went at a survivable speed. Then went even slower to take pics of all the beautiful scenery.
Animals spotted: Hawk (don't), Deer, Rabbit, Wild Goats, Domestic Goats, Horses, Many small birbs, Many dogs, Many sheep, Many cows, Bee.

column3: Return from Decathlon: Running Into Oasis Fans Edition
column5: grocery shopping also got logged

Went out to test camping set up. Left a bit late so pitched in pitch dark (wahey) near a reservoir.
Saw a badger for the first time in my life, and also two rabbits that just vibed on the road surprisingly long.
One uphill felt more like a pile of rocks than gravel, and then descent had "steps", so decent amount of walking was involved.

Woke up seemingly before the world started rendering, brewed some coffees and headed back. I think my cycling computer or my route planning messed up and went back more directly than intended, so did an extra arthur's seat loop and saw the burn damage
In protest of 3 drivers crashing into cyclists in the past week
with some rain
